item.title: Japan Inc.’s Shake-Up Sparks Flashbacks to Dot.com Boom and Bust
item.description: The rise to the top by AI-related companies has parallels to an earlier bubble

item.title: CoreWeave Plans European Investor Calls For Potential Debt Sale
item.description: CoreWeave Inc. is to hold calls with European high-yield investors on Tuesday as the AI infrastructure provider considers financing transactions that may include dollar and euro bonds.

item.title: Ten Years On, the UK Is Counting the Economic Cost
item.description: A decade after the Brexit vote, the UK is assessing the damage and debating how to unwind it.

item.title: Booming AI Demand Fuels Jump in China’s Exports
item.description: Bloomberg Economics cites link between global tech supercycle and trade flows

item.title: Standard Bots Raises $200 Million to Manufacture Robots in US
item.description: Standard Bots has raised $200 million in a new round of funding to ramp up manufacturing of robotic arms in the US as the country vies to keep pace with China in building more advanced robots.

item.title: Crop and Fertilizer Markets Wipe Out Iran War Risk Premium
item.description: The Iran war risk premium that swept through crop and fertilizer markets is rapidly evaporating as fears of prolonged supply disruptions fade, easing one of the biggest threats to food inflation.

item.title: Robots May Help Defuse US-China Tensions
item.description: The two countries have vital tech for the coming wave of robots

item.title: Sugar Swings as Traders Weigh Brazil Output Against Deficit Risk
item.description: Sugar futures fluctuated in New York as traders weighed ample Brazilian supplies against mounting El Niño risks that could tighten global supplies later this year.

item.title: Blackstone Is Buying Up SRTs as Banks Rush to Hedge Loan Risks
item.description: Blackstone Inc. is pushing forward in the fast-growing market for significant risk transfers as banks hedge possible losses in their swelling loan books.

item.title: Philippines’ PLDT Seeks Up to $400 Million From REIT Listing
item.description: Top Philippine telecoms firm PLDT Inc. plans to raise between $300 million and $400 million from the listing of a real estate investment trust for its data center business amid a global wave of investor interest in technology and artificial intelligence assets.

item.title: Taiwan Mulls Curbs on AI Chip Exports to China to Align With US
item.description: Taiwan authorities are considering much stricter export controls on AI chip sales to China to further align with US measures, according to people familiar with the matter, an effort to address semiconductor smuggling that risks drawing a rebuke from Beijing.

item.title: Paramount’s $110 Billion Warner Takeover Investigated by UK
item.description: Britain’s merger watchdog has opened a probe into Paramount Skydance Corp.’s $110 billion takeover of Warner Bros. Discovery Inc.

item.title: Telemundo, Fox Aim for Viewer Records With World Cup in the US
item.description: The start of the FIFA World Cup is just two days away, but for Comcast Corp.’s Telemundo, it’s been more than two years in the making. The Miami-based television network, which holds the Spanish-language rights to the tournament in the US, has long been preparing to lure in viewers with the idea that “soccer is more fun en Español.”

item.title: US Small-Business Optimism Falls to Lowest Since October 2024
item.description: An index of US small business optimism fell in May to the lowest level since October 2024, erasing almost all of the gains seen since President Donald Trump was elected for a second term.

item.title: Markets Prepare for Arrival of Musk’s AI-Infused Space Conglomerate
item.description: Markets prepare to welcome Elon Musk’s multiplanetary, AI-infused version of General Electric.

item.title: Kuwait Offers Oil to Asian Buyers for First Time Since War Began
item.description: Kuwait is offering to sell its crude to refiners in Asia for the first time since the Iran war started, the latest indication that oil flows from Persian Gulf producers are opening up despite Tehran’s threat to shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: StoneX Plans to Deepen India Presence Beyond First Trading Desk
item.description: StoneX Group Inc. plans to expand into base metals and agricultural commodities after setting up its first precious metals trading desk in India in June, according to top executives, deepening its presence in the fast-growing economy after serving it for years from overseas hubs.

item.title: Anthropic Calls Mythos Dangerous. Why Is It Expanding Access?
item.description: Anthropic PBC has said its new artificial intelligence tool, Mythos, is so good at finding vulnerabilities in software and computer systems that it can’t be released to the general public. The AI giant initially released Mythos to a small group of carefully chosen parties because if a tool this powerful fell into the wrong hands, Anthropic says, it could help attackers more easily steal data or disrupt critical infrastructure.

item.title: China’s Trade Imbalance With EU Swells as Imports Slip Again
item.description: China’s imports from the European Union dropped for the first time in three months, again swelling a trade imbalance that’s put it on a collision course with the bloc.

item.title: Bond Backed by French SME Loans Looks to Open a New Debt Market
item.description: French state-backed lender Bpifrance SACA is selling a new type of bond backed by small company loans, looking to end a more than decade-long regulatory impasse over European Secured Notes.

item.title: Uber-Backed Wayve Eyes Stake Sale on the UK’s New Private Market
item.description: Wayve Technologies Ltd., the UK-based autonomous driving software startup, is considering letting investors sell shares through the country’s new private securities market, according to people familiar with the matter, in what would be the first major endorsement of the novel system.

item.title: Colombia’s Beleaguered Oil Sector Eyeing Comeback in Venezuela
item.description: In a reversal of fortune, oil companies in Colombia, where outgoing president Gustavo Petro has all but barred exploration for the last four years, are scoping out Venezuela.

item.title: Brookfield, GIP Defy War to Advance $7.5 Billion Kuwait Deal
item.description: BlackRock Inc.’s Global Infrastructure Partners and Brookfield Asset Management Ltd. are among private equity firms shortlisted to buy a stake in a Kuwaiti pipeline network, indicating sustained interest for marquee Gulf assets despite a monthslong war that’s impacted regional production and roiled energy markets globally.

item.title: India’s Top Bank in $1 Billion Funding for Sun Pharma’s US Deal
item.description: State Bank of India plans to join a group of global lenders in funding Sun Pharmaceutical Industries Ltd.’s $12 billion overseas acquisition, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Ukraine Estimates $11 Billion in Farm Losses From Russia Strikes
item.description: Ukraine’s agricultural sector suffered direct losses of more than $11 billion as Russian drone strikes intensified sharply at the end of last year, according to Deputy Economy Minister Taras Vysotskyi.

item.title: Iconiq-Backed IT Startup NinjaOne Hits $12.3 Billion Valuation
item.description: NinjaOne, an IT management platform, has more than doubled its valuation to $12.3 billion in a new financing deal, bucking the trend of investors shying away from software-as-a-service companies over AI fears. The startup is set to announce Tuesday that it’s raised $400 million in a secondary share sale. Wellington Management, Iconiq Capital, Alphabet Inc.’s CapitalG and Sequoia Capital participated in the deal, which saw some early investors and longtime employees sell equity. NinjaOne was prev

item.title: South African Economic Growth Beats Forecasts Despite War Hit
item.description: South Africa’s economy grew at a faster pace in the first quarter, shrugging off the initial headwinds from the Iran war even as manufacturing continued to lag.

item.title: Bank of Canada Likely to Hold After Weak First Quarter Data: Decision Guide
item.description: The Bank of Canada is widely expected to continue holding its policy interest rate for a fifth consecutive meeting, after recent data pointed to a weaker economy than the central bank had projected.

item.title: Intesa CEO Braces for Potential Bidding War Over Monte Paschi
item.description: Intesa Sanpaolo SpA Chief Executive Officer Carlo Messina signaled he’s prepared to address any counterbids for Banca Monte dei Paschi di Siena SpA, after unveiling an offer that valued the world’s oldest bank at more than €30 billion ($35 billion).

item.title: Emerging-Market Stocks Rise Most in Two Months on AI Dip-Buying
item.description: Emerging-market stocks rebounded, led by South Korea, as investors snapped up artificial intelligence shares at lower prices after Monday’s selloff. Developing-nation currencies advanced after Iran and Israel paused hostilities.
